commit.message:
Implement theme system (Light / Dark / System default)

Wires the existing Settings > Theme dropdown to actual visual changes:
- Four CoffeeSettings colour accessors (ThemePanelBg, ThemeTextColor,
  ThemeDimTextColor, ThemeOutlineColor) return palette values per theme
- SyncAllWindows() broadcasts MSG_THEME_CHANGED to all open windows
- Custom Draw() methods (ParticleGaugeView, SieveDistView, CalHistView,
  ExtractionBarView, RoastGaugeView, ThumbView) use theme colours for
  backgrounds, outlines, tick marks, and labels
- BTextView tips areas update view/text colour on MSG_THEME_CHANGED and
  in constructors so newly opened windows inherit the active theme
- Coffee-colour gradients and extraction zone colours are unchanged

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

v0.4.0: Phase 1 — Person-Room BLE Tracking via Bermuda

Add per-person room localization using Bermuda BLE trilateration.
Each person's sensor.hai_person_* now shows which room they're in,
with confidence, source, and distance. Motion fallback when BLE
is unavailable.

Changes:
- config.yaml: Add bermuda_sensors option (JSON mapping person→sensor)
- main.py: BLE tracking in on_state_change, person room publishing
  with motion fallback, bermuda config parsing in main()
- features.py: New BLE features (ble_area_known, ble_distance,
  minutes_in_current_room) for person ML models
- ml_engine.py: Sync person room data from SensorEngine, pass
  BLE features through training and prediction pipelines
- mqtt_publisher.py: Fix stale sw_version (0.3.2→0.4.0)
- Dockerfile: Version label bump to 0.4.0
- docs: Phase 1 implementation plan

feat: v13.0 BRAIN 자본배분 중앙 두뇌 — Phase 1

NIGHTWATCH+VIX+충격유형 → ARM별 동적 자본배분 엔진.
기존 3개 ARM(스윙/ETF/인버스+현금)의 비중을 중앙에서 조율.

핵심 기능:
- VIX 6구간 동적 보정 (LOW→PANIC, 투자비중 ×0.30~×1.10)
- NIGHTWATCH 점수 기반 미세 보정 (채권 비토 시 레버리지 즉시 0)
- 충격 유형별 ARM 차등 축소 (GEOPOLITICAL/RATE/LIQUIDITY/COMPOUND)
- 안전장치: ±20%p 일일 변경 제한, 2일 레짐 확인 대기
- ETF 오케스트레이터와 연동: BRAIN 배분 → regime_allocation 오버라이드
- 텔레그램 브리핑 자동 생성

첫 실행 결과 (VIX 29.3, COMPOUND 충격):
  기본: 스윙30/섹터30/지수30/현금40
  BRAIN: 스윙22.5/섹터12.5/지수22.5/현금42.5 (투자 -17.5%p)

commit.message:
Add ADR 0027: Bashkit driver integration across Python, TypeScript, PHP

Documents the bashkit driver design: auto-resolution between native
extensions (PyO3/napi-rs/ext-php-rs) and IPC fallback (JSON-RPC over
stdin/stdout), host-owned VFS sync model, and bidirectional custom
command callbacks for both transport paths.

feat(editor): Phase 3 kickoff — standalone editor scaffold, scene serialisation, inspector, commands (766 tests)

- Editor application (editor/) — separate binary with ImGui, dockspace, menu bar
- Scene serialisation (engine/scene/) — JSON save/load with security (entity limits, NaN rejection, path traversal, file size limits)

- ECS components — Name, Parent, Children in render_system.h
- Scene hierarchy panel — entity list with click-to-select, right-click create/delete
- Inspector panel — editable Transform/Transform3D/Name, display-only Sprite/Material3D
- Command system — undo/redo with 256-depth bounded stack
- Entity commands — CreateEntity/DestroyEntity with full component snapshot
- Viewport panel — placeholder (FBO rendering deferred)
- 28 new tests (766 total), zero warnings on Clang-18 and GCC-13
- 3 .context.md files — editor/, engine/scene/, updated engine/core/
- ADR: docs/architecture/adr-editor-architecture.md

commit.message:
feat(file): add file management API, deploy provider, and templates (#228)

* docs: add file deploy and template rendering design

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add Object Store methods to NATSClient interface

Add CreateOrUpdateObjectStore and ObjectStore to the NATSClient
interface for upcoming file deploy feature. Add replace directive
for local nats-client development.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)
Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add Object Store config, file-state KV, and file permissions

Add NATSObjects and NATSFileState config structs with builder
functions and JetStream setup. Add file:read and file:write
permissions to built-in roles. Add implementation plan for
file deploy and template rendering feature.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add file API spec, job types, and provider interface

Add OpenAPI spec for file management endpoints (upload, list, get,
delete) with generated server code. Add file.deploy.execute and
file.status.get operation constants, FileState struct for KV tracking,
and file provider interface with Deploy/Status methods plus mocks.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)
Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add file API handlers, deploy provider, and job client methods

File API handlers (Task 4): upload, list, get, delete endpoints with
Object Store backend. Includes HTTP wiring tests and RBAC tests for
all endpoints (36 tests).

File deploy provider (Task 7): SHA-based idempotent file deployment
with state tracking via file-state KV. Status checks for in-sync,
drifted, and missing files (12 tests).

Job client methods (Task 10): ModifyFileDeploy and QueryFileStatus
convenience methods following existing patterns (7 tests).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)
Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: wire file API, add template rendering, and node deploy endpoints

Server wiring (Task 5): Add GetFileHandler to ServerManager, Object
Store handle in natsBundle, conditional file handler registration.

Template rendering (Task 8): Go text/template with TemplateContext
providing Facts, Vars, and Hostname. 12 test cases covering
substitution, conditionals, range, and error handling.

Node API endpoints (Task 11): POST /node/{hostname}/file/deploy (202)
and POST /node/{hostname}/file/status (200) with OpenAPI spec,
generated code, handlers, HTTP wiring and RBAC tests (32 tests).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)
Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: wire file provider into agent and add CLI commands

Agent wiring (Task 9): Add fileProvider to Agent struct, file category
processor dispatch for deploy/status operations, startup wiring with
Object Store and file-state KV handles. Graceful nil-provider handling
when Object Store is not configured. 12 new agent tests.

CLI commands (Task 12): File management commands (upload, list, get,
delete) and node file commands (deploy, status) with flag handling,
display formatting, and broadcast support. SDK calls scaffolded as
TODOs pending SDK integration (Task 13).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)
Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: add file management feature documentation

Add feature page for file management covering upload, deploy, template
rendering, SHA-based idempotency, and status checking. Update
docusaurus navbar, configuration reference with nats.objects and
nats.file_state sections, and README features matrix.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)
Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(file): wire validateFileName, add 400 responses, close coverage gaps

Wire validateFileName into file get/delete handlers following the
validateHostname pattern. Add 400 responses to OpenAPI spec and
regenerate. Close test coverage gaps in deploy (corrupt state, invalid
mode), status (invalid JSON), and template (execution error).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)
Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(file): add multipart upload, template deploy, and test coverage

Wire file management end-to-end: multipart upload with force flag and
content-type support, template rendering with agent facts, node file
deploy/status CLI commands, and Object Store bucket creation.

Standardize HTTP test naming across all API domains (ValidationHTTP
for endpoints with input validation, HTTP for plain wiring, RBACHTTP
for auth). Add missing ValidationHTTP + RBACHTTP tests for node disk,
load, memory, OS, and uptime endpoints. Close coverage gaps in
provider/facts, agent GetFacts, file list/upload/deploy handlers.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(file): rename FileProvider to Service, fix lint and coverage

Rename FileProvider → Service and NewFileProvider → New to eliminate
the golangci-lint type-stutter warning (file.FileProvider → file.Service).
Add package comment to satisfy revive package-comments rule. Extract
reusable FactsAware/FactsFunc pattern into provider package. Close
remaining test coverage gaps with marshalJSON/unmarshalJSON hooks and
additional edge-case tests. Remove dead validation.Struct call from
PostFile handler. Standardize HTTP test method naming across all API
domains (ValidationHTTP for endpoints with validation tags, HTTP for
plain wiring tests).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)

commit.message:
Exclude imageAvailable from substantive-filter guard

imageAvailable: true matches 725K of 831K artworks — it's a modifier,
not a search filter. When claude.ai sends "null" strings for the real
filters (productionPlace, subject), coerceNull strips them to undefined,
leaving imageAvailable as the only surviving param. The old guard treated
this as "has a filter" and returned 725K default-ranked results silently.
Now the model gets a clear error prompting it to use semantic_search or
pass actual filter values.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
docs: 记录 v1.1 回测结果（双层框架失败分析）

结论：v2.1 最终收益 -14.37%，比 v1.0 的 -4.93% 更差。
HOT 天数从 36.4% 降至 16.9%（方向正确），但因 MA60 制度
检测滞后，在 2019 年 1 月底部反转时 BEAR 制度压制了买入，
切回 NEUTRAL 后又在高位过度买入，双向做错。
下一步：BEAR 制度不降 HOT 阈值（方案A）或改用仓位乘数（方案B）。

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(ml): add serde(default) to new wick features to prevent dataset reset on restart

Without #[serde(default)], old dataset samples (missing window_upper_shadow /
window_lower_shadow) fail to deserialize on startup → dataset silently resets
to 0 → ML must retrain from scratch on every deploy.

Also demote 'No tradeable market' log from WARN to DEBUG since it is expected
when all markets are past the max_window_progress threshold.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(graph): wire graph analytics, health check, relations browse, and graph-prompt config

- recordGraphWrite() now called on every write when GRAPH_ENABLED; populates graph_events table
- POST /v2/memories and /v2/memory.write responses include top-level relations[] (graph triples) when graph enabled
- GET /v2/health is now async with live Neo4j connectivity check (neo4jConnected, neo4jNodeCount, neo4jRelationCount via Cypher ping)
- New GET /v2/graph/relations?user_id=&run_id=&limit= — thin wrapper around MemoryGraph.getAll()
- New GET/PUT /v2/config/graph-prompt — runtime graph extraction prompt, persisted to SQLite, seeded from MEM0_GRAPH_CUSTOM_PROMPT
- createMemory() accepts customGraphPrompt, injects as graphStore.customPrompt
- Default MEM0_GRAPH_LLM_MODEL updated from gpt-4o-mini to gpt-4.1-mini
- OpenAPI: POST /v2/memories response fully documented; HealthDiagnostics schema extended; /graph/relations and /config/graph-prompt paths added
- Docs: ARCHITECTURE.md full rewrite (component table, request flows, LLM budget, analytics schema); README.md updated; CHANGELOG.md populated

Implement total cost service with ACA subsidy overlay

Adds calculate_total_cost() as a thin orchestration layer over calculate_emr()
and calculate_aca_subsidy(). Computes ACA MAGI at each sweep point, inserts the
ACA cliff as an exact boundary point via the new extra_boundary_points parameter,
and combines emr + emr_aca into total_cost_emr. 24 unit tests across 6 classes
covering both ORDINARY and PREFERENTIAL sweep modes.

commit.message:
Switch duplicate artist detection to JS-based accent matching

SQL translate() with parameterized accent strings wasn't working.
Now fetches all artists and does accent comparison in JavaScript
using Unicode NFD normalization, which reliably strips diacriticals.

commit.message:
Fix Peltier DPDT relay polarity, PID tuning, thermistor diagnosis

- BED_CUSTOM_PIN_STATE: confirmed HIGH (heating on startup) with comments
  documenting M42 inversion (S0→pin HIGH→heat, S255→pin LOW→cool)
- Configuration_adv.h: corrected DPDT relay polarity comments to match
  actual hardware behavior (HIGH=heating, LOW=cooling)
- temperature.cpp: restored original PELTIER_CONTROL_E0 logic with
  clarifying comments about M42 S-value inversion
- FIRMWARE_CHANGES_LOG.txt: appended v2.2 session log covering:
  TH0 thermistor disconnection diagnosis (-15C → MINTEMP shutdown),
  PID autotune results (Kp=222.72 Ki=25.08 Kd=494.43, ±0.25C at 40C),
  DPDT relay polarity investigation and fix

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add React and Vue wrapper packages for Web Components

- wrappers/react: Typed React wrappers with forwardRef, prop-to-attribute
  mapping, useRuknLanguage() and useRuknTheme() hooks
- wrappers/vue: Vue 3 Composition API wrappers with RuknPlugin for global
  registration, useRuknLanguage() and useRuknTheme() composables
- Both packages wrap all 12 custom elements with full TypeScript types
- Both provide tree-shakeable individual imports
- Prepared for npm as @ruknds/react and @ruknds/vue (not yet published)

commit.message:
feat(03-02): improve startup UX — pre-warm LLM, fix loading message

- Don't show "Downloading" on every launch, just "Loading..."
- Pre-warm LLM model on server start so first request is fast
- Keep model loaded for 30min between requests

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: register pointer filter directly on upperCanvas capture phase

document-level capture + target comparison was unreliable.
Registering on upperCanvasEl with {capture:true} ensures our filter
always fires before Fabric.js's bubble-phase listeners, guaranteeing
mouse/pen/touch blocking actually works.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix image prompts: auto-expand short GM prompts, scene description first

Two root cause fixes:

1. GM writes short prompts despite instructions (pattern locked in from history).
   Server now auto-detects prompts under 20 words and expands them via a fast
   gemini-2.0-flash text call, using the scene narrative, location, character,
   and mood as context. Produces a proper 40-50 word comma-separated noun-phrase
   prompt specific to the actual scene.

2. Style prefix was dominating the image prompt.
   Image models weight the BEGINNING of the prompt most heavily. Putting 30 words
   of generic art-direction first meant the scene-specific content had low weight.
   Fixed by: scene description FIRST, mood/context NEXT, style LAST (as a brief
   comma-separated suffix rather than a leading sentence).

Also: pass narrative text (first 500 chars) from client to image endpoint so the
expansion call has the full scene context to work with.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Remove infrastructure reference in fundamentals closing line

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(electron): quitAndInstall with force-kill fallback for updates

quitAndInstall stages the update, then process.exit(0) after 3s
as fallback if it hangs on unsigned macOS. This ensures the update
is actually installed on next launch.

commit.message:
fix: harden Ollama prompt and add fallback for unparseable responses (#28)

- Rewrite system prompt to force JSON output even for irrelevant text
  (llama3 was refusing with plain text when articles didn't match the symbol)
- Return neutral fallback (score=0, confidence=0.1) instead of throwing
  when JSON parsing fails — prevents ingestion worker from crashing

Co-authored-by: Abhishek Mandhare <abhishek.mandhare@yahoo.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

better-sqlite3をSupabaseに変更（Vercel対応）

Vercelのサーバーレス環境ではネイティブモジュール(better-sqlite3)が
ビルドできないため、Supabase(PostgreSQL)に切り替え。

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: critical React hooks violation in ReactionPicker crashing all messages

ReactionPicker was calling useEmojiManifestStore inside a ternary
(conditional hook call), violating React's Rules of Hooks. When serverId
was passed, the hook order changed between renders, crashing the entire
React component tree and preventing all messages from rendering.

Also: wrap renderCustomEmojis in try-catch, remove debug console.logs,
and use manifest subscription directly in MessageContent text rendering.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
